headline: Nike Cutting 1,400 More Jobs. The Stock Is Rising.
summary: Nike  told its employees worldwide that it is cutting more jobs as part of the final stretch of its Win Now action plan.  The footwear and athletic apparel company has been taking “deliberate steps to strengthen our foundation, sharpen how we compete, and build a model designed to deliver long-term profitable growth,” including aligning its teams, capabilities, and footprint for its future needs.  Nike’s shares were up 0.7% at $45.08 in late Thursday trading, after closing down 2% in regular trading.

headline: Lululemon investors sour on new CEO pick as the stock sinks
summary: Lululemon (LULU) announced on Wednesday that former Nike (NKE) executive Heidi O’Neill will become the brand's next CEO starting in September. The stock has fallen slightly since reporting this news. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma outlines the investor reaction and what this all means for Lululemon's turnaround plan.

headline: Nike vs. Lululemon: One Brand Is Losing Its Cool — and Investors Are Paying for It
summary: Nike (NYSE:NKE) and Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU) both reported earnings with North America struggling, China providing brightness, and tariffs compressing margins. Two athletic apparel giants face very different problems, with very different risks for investors. Wholesale Saves Nike. China Saves Lululemon. Nike’s quarter showed contradictions in the mix.
